metadata.title: Enzalutamide Monotherapy
metadata.indication: Enzalutamide for metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer or high-risk non-metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer.
metadata.partial_assessment_note: Any independently actionable entered value is assessed; omitted domains remain explicitly unassessed and never block a partial result.

clinical_governance.prescriptive_authority: Treatment plan must be initiated by the appropriately qualified Consultant specified in the NCCP source.
clinical_governance.dose_modification_note: Any dose modification or non-standard continuation should be discussed with a Consultant.
clinical_governance.disclaimer: Decision-support encoding of the current NCCP source. It does not replace the official NCCP regimen, clinical judgement, local policy, prescribing review or pharmacy verification.

toxicity_grade.label: Worst current enzalutamide-related toxicity

options.label: Grade 0 — No toxicity
options.description: No clinical or laboratory evidence of this adverse event.

options.label: Grade 1 — Mild
options.description: Mild or asymptomatic; observation or diagnostic review only; intervention generally not indicated.

options.label: Grade 2 — Moderate
options.description: Moderate; minimal, local or non-invasive intervention may be indicated; limits instrumental activities of daily living.

options.label: Grade 3 — Severe
options.description: Severe or medically significant but not immediately life-threatening; hospitalisation may be indicated; limits self-care activities of daily living.

toxicity_grade.assessment_guidance: Identify the exact adverse event, assess symptoms and functional impact, and apply the toxicity-specific CTCAE v5.0 definition. Do not grade solely from a generic severity impression.

seizure_or_pres.label: Seizure or posterior reversible encephalopathy syndrome (PRES)

strong_cyp2c8_inhibitor.label: Strong CYP2C8 inhibitor cannot be avoided

renal_band.label: Renal function category

options.label: CrCl <30 mL/min — no adjustment expected; limited data
Haemodialysis — no adjustment expected; specialist review

renal_band.assessment_guidance: Select the protocol-specific renal category matching the verified CrCl/eGFR. Exact numerical entry is not required because the NCCP decision is categorical.

output_templates.proceed: No encoded restriction was triggered in the entered domain(s). This is not an overall treatment clearance.
output_templates.coverage_gap: Only entered values were assessed; all omitted clinically relevant domains remain unassessed.
output_templates.consultant_review: The entered value requires protocol-specific clinical review.
output_templates.withhold: Withhold and reassess according to the official NCCP pathway.
output_templates.delay: Delay treatment and reassess according to the official NCCP pathway.

action.message: Discontinue enzalutamide for seizure or PRES.

rules.explanation: Discontinue enzalutamide for seizure or PRES.

action.message: Interrupt for one week or until Grade ≤2, then resume at the same dose or 120/80 mg based on clinical judgement.

rules.explanation: Interrupt for one week or until Grade ≤2, then resume at the same dose or 120/80 mg based on clinical judgement.

action.message: Interrupt until severe hypertension is adequately controlled.

rules.explanation: Interrupt until severe hypertension is adequately controlled.

action.message: Reduce enzalutamide to 80 mg daily when a strong CYP2C8 inhibitor cannot be avoided.

rules.explanation: Reduce enzalutamide to 80 mg daily when a strong CYP2C8 inhibitor cannot be avoided.

action.message: No adjustment is expected, but severe renal impairment/haemodialysis has limited clinical data.

rules.explanation: No adjustment is expected, but severe renal impairment/haemodialysis has limited clinical data.

action.message: Severe hepatic impairment is an NCCP exclusion.

rules.explanation: Severe hepatic impairment is an NCCP exclusion.

treatment.additional_requirement: Continue medical castration unless surgically castrated where specified in the NCCP source.

regular: Disease and safety monitoring according to treatment plan and NCCP source.

supportive_care.mapping_basis: The NCCP regimen classifies this oral SACT as minimal emetogenic risk; the linked oral minimal-to-low supportive-care guidance is used rather than a parenteral prescription sheet.
